explanation:
## Understanding US Visa Costs

Applying for a US tourist visa (B1/B2) involves several mandatory fees and costs. This calculator breaks down the total investment so you can budget properly before you apply.

### What's Included

**MRV Fee**: The Visa Application Fee is $185 USD per person, paid directly to the US consulate. This is mandatory and non-refundable.

**Local Processing Costs**: Depending on your location, you may incur costs for required documents, visa photos (typically 2x2 inch color photos), official document translations, and travel to your visa interview.

### How to Use This Calculator

1. Enter the current USD/ARS exchange rate
2. The calculator converts the base MRV fee to your local currency
3. Adds estimated processing and documentation fees
4. Shows your total out-of-pocket cost for the visa application

### Important Notes

- This is an estimate. Actual costs may vary by consulate location
- The MRV fee ($185 USD) is set by the US government and doesn't change
- Exchange rates fluctuate daily—use the current rate for accuracy
- Some services (expedited processing, additional translations) cost extra
- Always check the official US embassy website for current fees

## B1/B2 Visa Overview

The B1/B2 visa is for temporary visits to the US for tourism, business meetings, conferences, or family visits. If approved, it's typically valid for 10 years with unlimited entries. However, the length of your stay per visit is determined by the US immigration officer at the port of entry (usually up to 6 months).

faq.q: How much does a US B1/B2 tourist visa cost?
faq.a: The MRV (visa application fee) is $185 USD per person. Total cost also includes processing fees, passport photos, document translations, and any additional services.
The Machine Readable Visa (MRV) fee is the official visa application fee set by the US Department of State. It costs $185 USD per applicant and is non-refundable.

faq.q: How long is a B1/B2 visa valid?
faq.a: If approved, a B1/B2 visa is typically valid for 10 years with unlimited entries. Your actual stay in the US per visit is determined by the immigration officer (usually up to 6 months).
Yes, an in-person interview at the US embassy or consulate is mandatory for most B1/B2 applicants. You'll schedule it online through the official visa appointment system.

faq.q: What documents do I need for a US tourist visa?
faq.a: Required documents typically include: valid passport, completed DS-160 form, visa fee receipt, appointment confirmation, passport-style photo, proof of financial support, and evidence of ties to your home country (employment, property, family).

faq.q: How long does the B1/B2 visa application take?
faq.a: Processing time varies by consulate. You may receive a decision same-day or wait several days to weeks. Standard processing typically takes 3-5 business days after your interview.
The DS-160 is the Online Nonimmigrant Visa Application required for all B1/B2 applicants. It's completed and submitted online through the State Department website and takes 20-30 minutes to fill out.

faq.q: Do I need a US visa to visit the United States?
faq.a: It depends on your citizenship. Citizens of 40 visa-waiver countries can enter the US without a visa for up to 90 days. Citizens of all other countries must apply for a B1/B2 tourist visa.

faq.q: Can I extend my B1/B2 visa stay in the United States?
faq.a: You can request an extension by filing Form I-539 with USCIS before your authorized stay expires. Extensions are granted on a case-by-case basis and require valid reasons.

sources.name: U.S. Department of State - Bureau of Consular Affairs
